previous_statement: delta:=||P-Phi||<=C eta and ||P||<=1+C eta. Work in End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). From S=2Phi-I, Phi=(I+S)/2, so P-Phi=(I+SR)/2-(I+S)/2=S(R-I)/2. By submultiplicativity (node 1.1.2), delta=||P-Phi||<=||S|| ||R-I||/2<=3*(C eta)/2=C eta, using ||S||<=3 (node 1.1) and ||R-I||<=C eta (node 1.2). By the triangle inequality (node 1.1.2), ||P||<=||Phi||+||P-Phi||<=1+C eta, using ||Phi||=1 (node 1.1.1).
new_statement: delta:=||P-Phi||<=C eta and ||P||<=1+C eta. In End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2), from S=2Phi-I we have Phi=(I+S)/2, so P-Phi=(I+SR)/2-(I+S)/2=S(R-I)/2. By submultiplicativity (node 1.1.2), delta=||P-Phi||<=||S|| ||R-I||/2<=(3/2) C_2 eta, using ||S||<=3 (node 1.1) and ||R-I||<=C_2 eta (node 1.2). DEFINE the universal constant C:=(3/2) C_2; then delta<=C eta. (Kitaev's prop_P, refs/kitaev:530, independently gives delta<=||2Phi-I|| O(eta), the same O(eta).) Then by the triangle inequality (node 1.1.2), ||P||<=||Phi||+delta<=1+C eta, using ||Phi||=1 (node 1.1.1). Uses nodes 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.2.
